items.q: How does a population of organisms become better adapted to its environment over time?
items.correct: Individuals with advantageous heritable variations survive and reproduce more; their alleles increase in frequency over generations.
items.misconception: Individual organisms change during their lifetime in response to their environment and pass the changes on to offspring.
items.random_wrong: Organisms decide what features they need and grow them by force of will.
items.why: The Lamarckian 'inheritance of acquired characteristics' is intuitive — students think exercise-built muscles or learned skills get passed to children.

items.q: Where do plants get most of their biomass from?
items.correct: From carbon dioxide in the air — the carbon in plant tissue comes mostly from CO2 fixed during photosynthesis.
items.misconception: Plants get most of their mass from the soil through their roots.
items.random_wrong: Plants gain most of their mass directly from sunlight, which has weight.
items.why: Roots in soil look like the source of food/mass, but actually most plant dry mass is carbon from atmospheric CO2.

items.q: What makes antibodies effective against pathogens?
items.correct: They have a specific shape complementary to antigens on a particular pathogen, marking it for destruction or neutralising it.
items.misconception: Antibodies are general-purpose proteins that work against any pathogen.
items.random_wrong: Antibodies are tiny knives that physically cut pathogens apart.
items.why: Students don't grasp antibody specificity, often picturing them as generic 'germ killers' rather than precision tools matching one antigen.

items.q: Where does the resistance allele in bacteria originally come from?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A random mutation. “Bacteria 'try' to resist the drug” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Why did dark peppered moths become MORE common during the Industrial Revolution?
items.correct: Dark moths were camouflaged on sooty bark so survived to reproduce
items.random_wrong: Birds preferred to eat dark moths
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Dark moths were camouflaged on sooty bark so survived to reproduce. “Soot killed pale moths directly” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In year 1, 2% of a bacterial population is resistant. By year 5 after heavy antibiotic use, 80% are resistant. By how many PERCENTAGE POINTS has resistance increased?
items.why: The real figure is 78. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
